title: Soft Silky Middle Eastern Tofu Tagine
description: A pungent and pillowy Middle Eastern dish featuring tofu, this tagine is a flavorful and aromatic monsoon of spices, perfect for a special occasion, and is onion and garlic free for those with sensitivities.

ingredients.name: extra firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into 1 inch cubes

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the cumin, smoked paprika, coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Add the tofu to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium high heat.
Add the tofu to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes, chicken broth, and dried apricots to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the chopped parsley.
Serve the tagine hot over couscous or rice.
